summary: The U.S. and Iran are telling very different stories about their latest nuclear talks. President Trump said Wednesday that Iran agreed to the 'highest level' inspections of its nuclear sites. Hours earlier, an Iranian official insisted there were 'no detailed discussions on the nuclear issue.' The IAEA chief confirmed inspections will happen, but the conflicting accounts leave real uncertainty about what was actually agreed. Meanwhile, Congress passed a war powers resolution aimed at ending the Iran conflict — the first such measure against a president — though it is largely symbolic. The resolution adds political pressure on the White House as the two sides remain far apart.

headlines.title: U.S. and Iran offer conflicting accounts of nuclear discussions
headlines.summary: President Trump claims Iran agreed to 'highest level' nuclear inspections, while Iranian officials deny detailed nuclear talks; IAEA chief confirms inspections will occur.

framings.angle: Focuses on conflicting narratives between U.S. and Iran
Emphasizes IAEA confirmation of inspections despite contradictions
Highlights Rubio's Gulf charm offensive amid ally fears over deal

headlines.title: Hong Kong doctors perform world’s first robotic living-donor liver transplant
headlines.summary: University of Hong Kong surgeons completed 48 robotic living-donor liver transplants since June 2025 with no complications, marking a global first in microsurgery.

headlines.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
headlines.summary: The largely symbolic resolution passed Tuesday adds pressure on the White House to end the Iran conflict, marking Congress's first war powers measure against a president.

headlines.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo
headlines.summary: France announced its first confirmed Ebola case on Wednesday in a doctor who returned from Democratic Republic of Congo; health authorities are monitoring contacts.

headlines.title: Appeals court allows Trump administration expanded use of speedy deportations
headlines.summary: A federal appeals court Tuesday permitted the Trump administration to resume speedy deportations of undocumented migrants nationwide, not just near the border.

headlines.title: U.S. sanctions Lagos-based ISIS financier and three bureaux de change
headlines.summary: The United States sanctioned Mukhtar Adamu Muhammad, 35, and three bureaux de change in Nigeria for allegedly moving funds for ISIS as part of a global terror crackdown.

headlines.title: North Korea’s Kim unveils plans for new warships and nuclear-armed navy
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un vowed to modernize North Korea's navy with new warships and nuclear capabilities, though analysts question the investment's strategic value.

headlines.title: EU hosts Taliban officials for migrant return talks in Brussels
headlines.summary: An Afghan Taliban delegation met EU officials in Brussels Tuesday for the first time, aiming to facilitate repatriation of failed asylum seekers; rights groups protested.

headlines.title: China lures world to yuan, hobbling Western sanctions on Iran and Russia
headlines.summary: Iran and Russia use yuan for oil sales and trade, evading dollar transaction controls, as China promotes the currency as a sanctions-proof alternative.

headlines.title: Jaishankar and South Korean FM discuss deepening strategic partnership in Seoul
headlines.summary: India's External Affairs Minister and South Korea's Foreign Minister met in Seoul to advance trade, investment, and finance cooperation agreed during President Lee's April visit.

headlines.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs told
headlines.summary: A Yale investigator will tell UK MPs that the Foreign Office failed to act on genocide warnings in Sudan since 2024 due to pressure from the UAE.

headlines.title: Israel’s military caught in political no man’s land in Lebanon
headlines.summary: Trump wants operations in Lebanon to end, but Israel's public demands continued fighting, leaving the military in a political stalemate.
headlines.previousContext: Israel's military operations in Lebanon have continued amid conflicting political pressures from the U.S. and domestic audiences.
